the flask app using the app factory pattern and a create_app function is designed to be flexible and reusable. it provides server routes and defines some cli commands using @app.cli.command. it is possible to use a different create_app when running as a server vs when running a cli command. alternatively, any parameters can be passed to create_app when running as a cli command. this can be achieved by setting an environment variable each time you run the cli command. for example, you can run flask run and set FLASK_MY_VARIABLE=x to specify the environment. you can then read this environment variable and act on it. it is possible to do this in a way that you don't need to supply the environment variable and just run flask my_command while still either running a different create_app or having a parameter set so you know the app was invoked as a cli command. the environment variable can be set in the same way as before. the environment variable can be used to determine the create_app to use. the environment variable can be used to set parameters for the create_app. the environment variable can be used to determine the app to use. the environment variable can be used
